description:
You are an on-call operations engineer.  
An alert has come in that requires you to place the “orders” service into maintenance mode immediately.

Current filesystem layout:
1. /home/user/app/config/service.yaml  
2. /home/user/app/config/service.toml  
3. The directory /home/user/logs/ already exists and is writable by the current user.

Present configuration values (for your reference):
• /home/user/app/config/service.yaml contains a top-level key called “maintenance_mode” whose value is currently “false”.  
• /home/user/app/config/service.toml contains a top-level key called “maintenance_mode” whose value is currently “false”.  

Task:
1. Change the value of the key named maintenance_mode from false to true in BOTH files.  
   • YAML syntax must remain valid: after your edit the line must read exactly  
     maintenance_mode: true  
   • TOML syntax must remain valid: after your edit the line must read exactly  
     maintenance_mode = true  

2. Immediately record the action in the log file /home/user/logs/incident_maintenance_toggle.log.  
   • Create the file if it does not already exist.  
   • Append exactly TWO new lines, one per file you modified, in the order you updated them.  
   • Each line must follow this exact format (note the single space after the timestamp):  
     YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS <file-name> maintenance_mode toggled to true  
     Example:  
     2024-05-12 09:41:28 service.yaml maintenance_mode toggled to true  
   • The timestamp must be the current system date and 24-hour time at the moment you write the line, formatted precisely as shown above.

Success criteria checked by the automated grader:
• /home/user/app/config/service.yaml contains the exact string “maintenance_mode: true” (no surrounding quotes, indentation unchanged).  
• /home/user/app/config/service.toml contains the exact string “maintenance_mode = true” (no surrounding quotes, indentation unchanged).  
• /home/user/logs/incident_maintenance_toggle.log exists and its last TWO lines strictly match the required pattern and reference service.yaml and service.toml in that order.

Use a single terminal command (one invocation line) to complete both configuration edits and the logging step. No manual editing with interactive editors is allowed. If you need to chain tools, combine them with &&, ;, or | so that the whole solution stays on one command line.

Do not output any explanatory text—just run the command(s) needed in the terminal.
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins:

File: /home/user/app/config/service.yaml  
Contents (exact):  
```
# orders service configuration
maintenance_mode: false
port: 8080
```

File: /home/user/app/config/service.toml  
Contents (exact):  
```
# orders service configuration
maintenance_mode = false
port = 8080
```

Directory: /home/user/logs/  
– empty at start (may contain unrelated historical files, but incident_maintenance_toggle.log does NOT exist).

Verification details after task is finished:

1. /home/user/app/config/service.yaml must contain the line:  
   maintenance_mode: true  
   and MUST NOT contain the substring “maintenance_mode: false”.

2. /home/user/app/config/service.toml must contain the line:  
   maintenance_mode = true  
   and MUST NOT contain the substring “maintenance_mode = false”.

3. /home/user/logs/incident_maintenance_toggle.log must exist and end with exactly two lines.  
   Sample acceptable ending (timestamps will differ):  
   2024-05-12 09:41:28 service.yaml maintenance_mode toggled to true  
   2024-05-12 09:41:28 service.toml maintenance_mode toggled to true  

   • The grader reads only the FINAL two lines.  
   • It will validate:  
     – both lines use pattern ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} (service\.(yaml|toml)) maintenance_mode toggled to true$  
     – the first of the two lines mentions service.yaml, the second mentions service.toml.

The agent runs without root privileges; all referenced paths are writable by the current user. No other hidden requirements.
name: task_000000_8a2bf09c
